questionHtml: When the spheres move outward, the angular speed decreases from 20 rad s−1 to 12 rad s−1. Calculate the percentage change in rotational kinetic energy that occurs when the spheres move outward.
markschemeHtml:
«Ek =» 12Lω1 = 12Lω2 ✓

OR

Ek1Ek2=ω1ω2

OR

«L is constant so» Ek is proportional to ω ✓

40 % «energy loss» ✓

 

MP1 is for understanding that angular momentum is constant so change in rotational kinetic energy is proportional to change in angular velocity

Award [0] if E = 0.5 I ω2 is used with the same I value for both values of E

Award [2] for BCA
